content.full_text:
August 16, 1990

Ms. Sandra Taylor  
Political Reform Consultant  
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division  
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
428 "J" Street, Sixth Floor  
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR WRITTEN CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE

Dear Ms. Taylor:

This letter will confirm telephone advice you provided to me this date. I phoned to ask regarding application of the contribution limits of Proposition 73 with respect to contributions to a candidate by that candidate's spouse. You advised me that contributions from community property funds would be treated as if made by the candidate and therefore not subject to the limitations. (Government Code Section 85301.) However, you advised that contributions from a spouse's separate property would be subject to the contribution limitations (i.e., $1,000).

I asked you if this advice was contained in any existing advice letter or in any manual, since I was aware of no regulation on point. You researched this and called me back to say that there was no written document on point. However, you assured me this is the advice which Commission staff has been giving and was, in fact, supported by the Enforcement Division staff.

You and I agreed that it would be good to have such advice in writing. Consequently, I am writing this letter seeking written confirmation. In your response, please clarify whether it is only the candidate spouse's share of community property funds which is not subject to the limit, or whether it is all community property funds which are not subject to the limit.

I appreciate your assistance. A prompt response will be greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
